title: PersonalOntologyScoringSchema
description: Shared scoring schema for all personal ontology tests. All scored dimensions use a 0-5 integer scale. Applies to both predicted scores in example_items.jsonl and actual scores recorded during test runs.

useful_friction.description: Load-bearing resistance that reveals structure, requires mastery, or generates discovery payoff. 0=no such resistance; 5=deep, rewarding structural challenge.

waste_friction.description: Energy-leaking resistance from poor design, social noise, institutional obstruction, or arbitrary complexity. 0=none; 5=severe/intolerable.

channel_convergence.description: Degree to which multiple active channels align without requiring the system to reconcile contradictory signals. Applies to attraction, environments, and gestalt object responses. 0=channels actively conflict; 5=full convergence across all active channels.

interior_signal.description: Strength of recursive interiority signal — evidence that the object has an inhabited, self-authoring inside rather than a performed or scripted surface. 0=NPC/empty/purely scripted; 5=strong recursive self-presence.

boundary_clarity.description: Sharpness of the object's inside/outside definition. How well-bounded is the interior? 0=flat, boundaryless, no detectable inside; 5=crisp, navigable, well-defined interior with clear threshold.

continuity_preservation.description: Degree to which identity-bearing structure (invariants) is preserved across a transformation. 0=identity fully broken; 5=full continuity of essential invariants maintained.

transformation_tolerance.description: Amount of structural change the object absorbs before losing identity. Orthogonal to continuity_preservation: an object can tolerate high change while still preserving identity if invariants survive. 0=brittle, collapses on minimal change; 5=high tolerance for morphing while retaining core.

artificiality_penalty.description: Penalty applied when the object is detected as surface-only construction: social script, performed depth, AI slop, influencer aesthetics, generic production. 0=no penalty detected; 5=maximal rejection triggered.

inspection_deepening.description: Degree to which closer inspection rewards attention with more coherent, layered, or structurally consistent content. Surface acts as portal. 0=inspection adds nothing; 5=each inspection layer reveals more consistent interior.

inspection_collapse.description: Degree to which closer inspection reveals emptiness, inconsistency, or mask. Surface acts as performance. 0=no collapse; 5=inspection fully exposes surface-only construction.

overall_response.description: Net predicted or observed response. 0=rejection/repellent; 1=avoidance; 2=tolerance; 3=neutral/passive interest; 4=positive engagement; 5=peak attractor / addictive.

scoring_rules.inspection_note: inspection_deepening and inspection_collapse are mutually exclusive at high values. An item scoring 4-5 on one should score 0-1 on the other. Stable items (neither deepen nor collapse) score 2-3 on both or 0 on both.
scoring_rules.waste_useful_note: waste_friction and useful_friction are independent. An object can have both (useful challenge wrapped in frustrating UI). Record both.
scoring_rules.overall_note: overall_response is the only required dimension. All others are scored only for tests where they apply.
